ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for smoked salmon pizza or Singaporean-Italian fusion pizza. FOODON_00002572 (pizza food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pizza) is the closest parent category. No specific code exists for smoked salmon pizza or Peranakan-Italian fusion pizza variants.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific fusion pizza dish. SNOMED CT does not have a granular food code for smoked salmon pizza.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Assam Smoked Salmon Pizza as served at Mingle - Funan, Singapore, weighing approximately 300 g, constituting a single-person meal portion.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Salmon, smoked (SR Legacy #15236) and Pizza, cheese, regular crust (SR Legacy #21299), combined with component estimation for assam aioli and curry leaves
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific fusion dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 40% pizza base with fior di latte (using USDA SR Legacy pizza cheese regular crust as proxy), 25% smoked salmon (USDA SR Legacy #15236), 15% assam fish aioli (estimated from mayonnaise-based aioli with tamarind, USDA SR Legacy), 10% red onions and cherry tomatoes (USDA SR Legacy), and 10% fried curry leaves (estimated from USDA SR Legacy curry leaves and frying oil). Values represent a plausible central estimate for a 100 g portion of the assembled dish. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; thin-crust pizza with cheese and toppings analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Assam Smoked Salmon Pizza. GI of 50 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for pizza with cheese and seafood toppings on a regular wheat crust (GI range 40–57 in published tables; Atkinson et al. 2021, Am J Clin Nutr). The protein and fat content from smoked salmon and fior di latte further attenuate glycemic response. GI 50 is a conservative central estimate. GL per serving = (50 × 20.5 × 300) / (100 × 100) = 30.75, rounded to 30.8. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1560 mg per full serving) from smoked salmon and aioli
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load from smoked salmon curing and aioli; a single serving may provide more than 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~31 per serving) from wheat pizza base, partially offset by protein and fat from smoked salmon and fior di latte
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this pizza in reduced portions, as the wheat-based crust contributes a moderate glycemic load per serving; the high protein and fat content from smoked salmon and mozzarella help blunt the postprandial glucose response compared to plain pizza.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from smoked salmon offset by high sodium and moderate saturated fat from fior di latte and aioli
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While smoked salmon provides cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), the high sodium content and moderate saturated fat from mozzarella and aioli are concerns for cardiovascular disease patients, who should limit portion size and frequency of consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Assam Smoked Salmon Pizza is a Singaporean-Italian fusion pizza served at Mingle - Funan in Singapore. It features a baked wheat pizza base topped with fior di latte (fresh mozzarella), cold-smoked salmon, a house-made assam fish aioli (a tamarind-spiced mayonnaise-based sauce drawing on Peranakan and Southeast Asian flavour traditions), sliced red onions, halved cherry tomatoes, and fried curry leaves as a garnish. The dish bridges Italian pizza-making technique with Southeast Asian flavour profiles: the tangy, slightly sour assam aioli contrasts with the rich, silky smoked salmon and the creamy fior di latte, while the fried curry leaves add an aromatic, slightly bitter crunch. The cherry tomatoes and red onions provide freshness and acidity. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy food (~218 kcal per 100 g) with meaningful protein from smoked salmon (~11.5 g per 100 g), moderate fat (~10.2 g per 100 g) including beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from the salmon, and a wheat-based carbohydrate base (~20.5 g per 100 g). Sodium content is high (~520 mg per 100 g) due to the smoked salmon and aioli, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 50 (low GI), though the glycemic load per full serving is moderate at approximately 31. The dish contains gluten, dairy, fish, and egg allergens and is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal certification without verification.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for this specific fusion dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA SR Legacy entries for smoked salmon, cheese pizza, aioli, vegetables, and curry leaves. GI is estimated from analogous pizza entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are individually well-supported in USDA SR Legacy, and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. This dish is a Singaporean-Italian fusion pizza served exclusively at Mingle - Funan (field survey data, 1 restaurant). The cuisine tag 'Italian' in the input is partially correct but the dish is better classified as Singaporean-Italian Fusion due to the assam aioli and curry leaf elements. 2. Nutrient values are component-estimated; a direct laboratory analysis or HPB Foodo entry would improve confidence to high. 3. Sodium is high due to smoked salmon curing process; the assam aioli also contributes sodium. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not -1 sentinel values; they are plausible estimates. No sentinel -1 values are used in this record. 5. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database pizza entries; gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. GL per serving = (50 × 20.5 × 300) / 10000 = 30.75, reported as 30.8. 6. Halal status is unconfirmed; smoked salmon and aioli ingredients require halal certification verification. 7. The food_name has been refined to 'Assam Smoked Salmon Pizza' to reflect the pizza format described in the dish description (fior di latte base, toppings consistent with pizza). 8. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Singapore-served dish. 9. All aliases_by_language entries are confirmed present verbatim in the flat aliases list.
